content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 4 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 101v–102v | **Source Images:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/DEU_004_page_001.jpg, DEU_004_page_002.jpg, DEU_004_page_003.jpg; source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/DEU_004_vaticanus_page_001.jpg, DEU_004_vaticanus_page_002.jpg

---

**1** "And now, Israel — listen to the statutes and the ordinances I am about to teach you. Follow them so that you will live, and so that you will go in and take possession of the land the LORD, the God of your fathers, is giving you. **2** Do not add anything to what I command you, and do not take anything away from it — but keep the commands of the LORD your God that I am giving you.

**3** "Your own eyes have seen what the LORD did at Baal-peor. The LORD your God destroyed from among you everyone who followed Baal of Peor. **4** But all of you who held fast to the LORD your God are alive today — every one of you.

**5** "See — I have taught you statutes and ordinances just as the LORD my God commanded me, so that you may follow them in the land you are entering to possess. **6** Keep them and carry them out, for this will show your wisdom and discernment to the nations. When they hear about all these statutes, they will say: 'Surely this great nation is a wise and discerning people.' **7** For what great nation has a god as close to it as the LORD our God is close to us whenever we call on him? **8** And what great nation has statutes and ordinances as righteous as all this instruction I am setting before you today?

**9** "Only be careful — watch yourself closely, so you do not forget the things your eyes have seen, and so they do not slip from your heart for the rest of your life. Teach them to your children and your grandchildren — **10** especially the day you stood before the LORD your God at Horeb. The LORD said to me: 'Gather the people to me so I may let them hear my words — so they will learn to fear me all the days they live on the earth, and so they will teach their children.'

**11** "You came and stood at the foot of the mountain, and the mountain was ablaze with fire to the very heart of heaven — darkness, cloud, and thick gloom. **12** Then the LORD spoke to you from the fire. You heard the sound of words, but you saw no form — only a voice.

**13** "He declared to you his covenant — the Ten Words — which he commanded you to keep, and he wrote them on two stone tablets. **14** The LORD commanded me at that time to teach you statutes and ordinances so you could follow them in the land you are crossing over to possess.

**15** "Be extremely careful — because you saw no form when the LORD spoke to you from the fire at Horeb — **16** so you do not act corruptly and make for yourselves an idol, a carved image of any form whatsoever: the shape of a man or a woman, **17** the shape of any animal on the earth, the shape of any bird that flies in the sky, **18** the shape of anything that crawls on the ground, the shape of any fish in the water below the earth. **19** And when you look up to the sky and see the sun and the moon and the stars — all the heavenly host — do not be drawn away to bow down to them and serve them. The LORD your God has apportioned these to all the peoples under heaven. **20** But you — the LORD took you and brought you out of Egypt, out of that iron-smelting furnace, to be a people for his very own inheritance — as you are today.

**21** "The LORD was angry with me because of you and swore that I would not cross the Jordan or enter the good land the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance. **22** I am going to die in this land; I will not cross the Jordan. But you are going to cross it and possess that good land. **23** Be careful not to forget the covenant of the LORD your God that he made with you, or make yourselves an idol — any image of any kind whatsoever — for the LORD your God has forbidden it. **24** For the LORD your God is a consuming fire, a jealous God.

**25** "After you have had children and grandchildren, and you have grown old in the land — if you act corruptly and make an idol of any kind, doing what is evil in the sight of the LORD your God and provoking him to anger — **26** I call heaven and earth as witnesses against you today: you will very quickly vanish from the land you are crossing the Jordan to possess. You will not live long in it; you will be completely destroyed. **27** The LORD will scatter you among the nations, and only a few of you will survive among the nations where the LORD drives you. **28** There you will serve gods made by human hands — wood and stone — gods that can neither see nor hear nor eat nor smell.

**29** "But if from there you seek the LORD your God, you will find him — when you search with all your heart and all your being. **30** When you are in distress and all these things have happened to you — in the final days you will return to the LORD your God and listen to his voice. **31** For the LORD your God is a compassionate God. He will not abandon you or destroy you. He will not forget the covenant he swore to your fathers.

**32** "Now search through all of history — from the day God created human beings on the earth, from one end of heaven to the other: Has anything so great as this ever happened? Has anything like it ever been heard of? **33** Has any people heard the voice of God speaking out of the fire — as you heard it — and lived? **34** Has any god ever attempted to take a nation for himself out of the midst of another nation, with trials, signs, wonders, and war, with a mighty hand and an outstretched arm, and with great terrors — like everything the LORD your God did for you in Egypt before your very eyes?

**35** "You were shown all this so that you would know: the LORD — he is God. There is no other besides him. **36** From heaven he let you hear his voice to discipline you. On earth he showed you his great fire, and his words came from the midst of the fire. **37** Because he loved your fathers and chose their descendants after them, he brought you out of Egypt himself, by his great power, **38** to drive out before you nations greater and mightier than you, to bring you in, and to give you their land as an inheritance — as it is today.

**39** "Know it today and take it to heart: the LORD is God in heaven above and on the earth below. There is no other. **40** Keep his statutes and his commandments, which I am giving you today, so that it will go well with you and your children after you — so that you will live long in the land the LORD your God is giving you for all time."

**41** Then Moses set apart three cities in the east, beyond the Jordan, **42** to which someone who killed a person could flee — someone who killed their neighbor unintentionally, without having hated them beforehand. They could flee to one of these cities and live. **43** They were: Bezer in the wilderness, on the plateau, for the Reubenites; Ramoth in Gilead for the Gadites; and Golan in Bashan for the Manassehites.

**44** This is the instruction Moses set before the sons of Israel. **45** These are the testimonies, statutes, and ordinances Moses spoke to the sons of Israel after they came out of Egypt — **46** beyond the Jordan, in the valley opposite Beth-peor, in the land of Sihon king of the Amorites, who reigned in Heshbon, whom Moses and the sons of Israel defeated when they came out of Egypt. **47** They seized his land and the land of Og king of Bashan — the two Amorite kings' lands beyond the Jordan, to the east — **48** from Aroer, which is on the edge of the Arnon Gorge, as far as Mount Sirion (that is, Hermon), **49** together with all the Arabah east of the Jordan, as far as the Sea of the Arabah below the slopes of Pisgah.

---

## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Dynamic choices — PNEUMA:**

**V.2 — "Do not add anything...do not take anything away":** This is a fundamental principle of canonical fidelity stated at the very opening of Moses' teaching. It will recur. PNEUMA preserves it verbatim — the boundary around the words is itself a word.

**V.6 — "this will show your wisdom and discernment to the nations":** Moses frames Israel's obedience as a theological witness to the world — living by these laws is not merely personal piety but public testimony. PNEUMA renders this missiological force clearly.

**V.7 — "a god as close to it as the LORD our God is close to us whenever we call on him":** The argument from divine nearness (qerovim elav) is philosophically distinctive: other nations' gods are remote, but Israel's God answers prayer. PNEUMA keeps this comparative argument sharp.

**V.12 — "you heard the sound of words, but you saw no form — only a voice":** The prohibition on idolatry is grounded here in the founding theophany: at Horeb, Israel heard but did not see. Therefore no image can represent what was never visually disclosed. PNEUMA renders "qol devarim" — literally "voice of words" — as "the sound of words" to preserve both dimensions.

**V.20 — "that iron-smelting furnace":** Hebrew kur ha-barzel — the iron furnace — is an image of brutal metallurgical process. Egypt was not merely bondage; it was a place of refinement through suffering. PNEUMA's "iron-smelting furnace" is visceral and industrial, capturing the horror of Egyptian slavery.

**V.24 — "a consuming fire, a jealous God":** These two divine attributes stand in creative tension: the fire that destroys corruption and the jealousy (qanna) that protects relationship. PNEUMA preserves both without domesticating either.

**V.29 — "when you search with all your heart and all your being":** Hebrew be-khol levavkha uve-khol nafshekha — with your whole heart and your whole nephesh (self/life-breath). This anticipates the Shema of chapter 6. PNEUMA renders "being" for nephesh here — fuller than "soul" but less clinical than "self."

**Vv.32-34 — rhetorical questions as cosmic argument:** Moses builds to a climax through a series of rhetorical questions spanning all history and all space. PNEUMA renders these with their full oratorical force — this is Moses the orator at his most magnificent.

**V.39 — "Know it today and take it to heart":** Hebrew veyadata ha-yom va-hashevota el levavekha — "know today and return it to your heart." The Hebrew image is of actively bringing knowledge back into the center of one's being. PNEUMA's "take it to heart" is idiomatic but preserves the volitional quality.

**Vaticanus (LXX) observation:** Vaticanus pages 146–147 cover this chapter. At v.19 the LXX reads "emeiren kyrios ho theos sou auta pasin tois ethnesin" — "the LORD your God divided/apportioned these to all the nations." The verb "emerisen" (apportioned) is striking and exactly matches the MT's "chalaq." At v.24 the LXX reads "theos zelotes" — "a jealous/zealous God" — preserving the Hebrew qanna. The theological weight of divine jealousy as a relational category is maintained in the Greek. Vaticanus page 146 is clearly paginated and shows the transition from chapter 3 through the early verses of chapter 4.
